From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Honorific style

"His Excellency" and "Her Excellency" redirect here. For other uses, see [His Excellency (disambiguation)](/wiki/His_Excellency_(disambiguation) "His Excellency (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/89/Their_Excellencies_the_Lords_Justices_of_England%2C_for_the_administration_of_the_Government_during_the_absence_of_the_King_by_Robert_White.jpg/250px-Their_Excellencies_the_Lords_Justices_of_England%2C_for_the_administration_of_the_Government_during_the_absence_of_the_King_by_Robert_White.jpg)](/wiki/File:Their_Excellencies_the_Lords_Justices_of_England,_for_the_administration_of_the_Government_during_the_absence_of_the_King_by_Robert_White.jpg)

Their Excellencies the Lords Justices of England, for the administration of the Government during the absence of the [King](/wiki/William_III_of_England "William III of England") by [Robert White](/wiki/Robert_White_(engraver) "Robert White (engraver)").

**Excellency** is an [honorific](/wiki/Honorific "Honorific") [style](/wiki/Style_(manner_of_address) "Style (manner of address)") given to certain high-level officers of a [sovereign state](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state"), officials of an [international organization](/wiki/International_organization "International organization"), or members of an [aristocracy](/wiki/Aristocracy "Aristocracy"). Once entitled to the title "Excellency", the holder usually retains the right to that courtesy throughout their lifetime,[[1]](#cite_note-1) although in some cases the title is attached to a particular office and is held only during tenure of that office.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Generally people addressed as *Excellency* are [heads of state](/wiki/Heads_of_state "Heads of state"), [heads of government](/wiki/Heads_of_government "Heads of government"), [governors](/wiki/Governor "Governor"), [ambassadors](/wiki/Ambassador "Ambassador"), [Roman Catholic bishops](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_bishops "Roman Catholic bishops"), high-ranking ecclesiastics, and others holding equivalent rank, such as heads of international organizations.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Members of [royal families](/wiki/Royal_families "Royal families") generally have distinct addresses such as [Majesty](/wiki/Majesty "Majesty"), [Highness](/wiki/Highness "Highness"), etc..

While not a title of office itself, the honorific *Excellency* precedes various titles held by the holder, both in speech and in writing. In reference to such an official, it takes the form *His* or *Her Excellency*; in direct address, *Your Excellency*, or, less formally, *Excellency*.
The abbreviation **HE** is often used instead of *His/Her Excellency*; alternatively it may stand for *[His Eminence](/wiki/Eminence_(style) "Eminence (style)")/[Her Eminence](/wiki/Eminence_(style) "Eminence (style)")*.

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Government")]

### Heads of state and government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Heads of state and government")]

In most [republican](/wiki/Republic "Republic") [nation states](/wiki/Nation_state "Nation state"), the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") is formally referred to as *His* or *Her Excellency*.

If a republic has a separate [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government"), that official is almost always addressed as *Excellency* as well. If the nation is a [monarchy](/wiki/Monarchy "Monarchy"), however, the customs may vary. For example, in the case of Australia, all ambassadors, high commissioners, state governors and the governor-general and their spouses are entitled to the use of Excellency.

Governors of colonies in the [British Empire](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire") were entitled to be addressed as *Excellency* and this remains the position for the governors of what are now known as [British Overseas Territories](/wiki/British_Overseas_Territories "British Overseas Territories").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### International diplomacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: International diplomacy")]

In various [international organizations](/wiki/International_organization "International organization"), notably the UN and its agencies, *Excellency* is used as a generic form of address for all republican [heads of state](/wiki/Heads_of_state "Heads of state") and [heads of government](/wiki/Heads_of_government "Heads of government"). It is often granted to the organization's head as well, and to those chiefs of UN diplomatic missions, such as [Resident Coordinators](/wiki/Resident_Coordinator "Resident Coordinator") (who are the designated representatives of the secretary-general), who are accredited at the head of state level (like an ambassador), or at the lower head of government level.

### International judiciary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: International judiciary")]

Judges of the [International Court of Justice](/wiki/International_Court_of_Justice "International Court of Justice") also enjoy the style of *Excellency*.

## Monarchy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Monarchy")]

### Royalty

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Royalty")]

In some monarchies the [husbands](/wiki/Husband "Husband"), [wives](/wiki/Wife "Wife"), or children of a [royal prince](/wiki/Royal_prince "Royal prince") or princess, who do not possess a princely title themselves, may be entitled to the style. For example, in [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") spouses or children of a born *[infante](/wiki/Infante "Infante")* or *[infanta](/wiki/Infanta "Infanta")* are addressed as *Excellency*, if not accorded a higher style.

Former members of a royal house or family, who have forfeited a royal title, may be awarded the style afterwards. Examples are former husbands or wives of a royal prince or princess, including [Alexandra, Countess of Frederiksborg](/wiki/Alexandra,_Countess_of_Frederiksborg "Alexandra, Countess of Frederiksborg"), following her divorce from [Prince Joachim of Denmark](/wiki/Prince_Joachim_of_Denmark "Prince Joachim of Denmark"). Likewise, [Count Carl Johan Bernadotte of Wisborg](/wiki/Count_Carl_Johan_Bernadotte_of_Wisborg "Count Carl Johan Bernadotte of Wisborg"), who lost his succession rights to the [Swedish throne](/wiki/Monarch_of_Sweden "Monarch of Sweden") and discontinued use of his royal titles in 1946 when he married the commoner Elin Kerstin Margaretha Wijkmark, was accorded the style.

In some [emirates](/wiki/Emirate "Emirate"), such as [Kuwait](/wiki/Kuwait "Kuwait") and [Qatar](/wiki/Qatar "Qatar"), the Emir, heir apparent and prime minister are called *His Highness*. Their children are styled with *His/Her Excellency* unless they possess a higher honorific.

### Nobility

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Nobility")]

In Spain members of the high nobility, holding the dignity of [grandee](/wiki/Grandee "Grandee"), are referred to as *The Most Excellent Lord/Lady*.

In Denmark, some [counts](/wiki/Count "Count") (*lensgrever*), historically those related by blood or marriage to the [Danish monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Denmark "Monarchy of Denmark"), who have entered a [morganatic marriage](/wiki/Morganatic_marriage "Morganatic marriage") or otherwise left the [Royal Family](/wiki/Danish_royal_family "Danish royal family") have the right to be styled as *Your Excellency*, e.g., the Counts of [Danneskiold-Samsøe](/wiki/Danneskiold-Sams%C3%B8e "Danneskiold-Samsøe"), some of the [counts of Rosenborg](/wiki/Count_of_Rosenborg "Count of Rosenborg") and the [Countess of Frederiksborg](/wiki/Alexandra,_Countess_of_Frederiksborg "Alexandra, Countess of Frederiksborg") (*[ad personam](/wiki/Ad_personam "Ad personam")*).

In the subnational monarchy [Sultanate of Sulu](/wiki/Sultanate_of_Sulu "Sultanate of Sulu"), senior nobility and holders of royal offices that are granted the title of [Datu Sadja](/wiki/Datu_Sadja "Datu Sadja") are referred to as *His/Her Excellency*.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Knights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Knights")]

*Excellency* can also attach to a prestigious quality, notably in an order of knighthood. For example, in the [Empire of Brazil](/wiki/Empire_of_Brazil "Empire of Brazil"), it was attached to the highest classes, each time called *[Grand Cross](/wiki/Grand_Cross "Grand Cross")*, of all three imperial orders: [Imperial Order of Pedro I](/wiki/Order_of_Pedro_I "Order of Pedro I"), [Imperial Order of the Southern Cross](/wiki/Order_of_the_Southern_Cross "Order of the Southern Cross") with the military honours of a [Lieutenant general](/wiki/Lieutenant_general "Lieutenant general") and [Order of the Rose](/wiki/Order_of_the_Rose "Order of the Rose").

Knights of the Collar and Knights Grand Cross of the Spanish Orders of Chivalry, such as the [Order of Charles III](/wiki/Order_of_Charles_III "Order of Charles III"), [Order of Isabella the Catholic](/wiki/Order_of_Isabella_the_Catholic "Order of Isabella the Catholic"), [Order of Civil Merit](/wiki/Order_of_Civil_Merit "Order of Civil Merit"), [Order of Alfonso X the Wise](/wiki/Civil_Order_of_Alfonso_X,_the_Wise "Civil Order of Alfonso X, the Wise"), [Royal Order of Sports Merit](/wiki/Royal_Order_of_Sports_Merit "Royal Order of Sports Merit"), [Civil Order of Health](/wiki/Civil_Order_of_Health "Civil Order of Health"), as well as recipients of the Grand Cross of [Military](/wiki/Crosses_of_Military_Merit_(Spain) "Crosses of Military Merit (Spain)"), [Naval](/wiki/Crosses_of_Naval_Merit_(Spain) "Crosses of Naval Merit (Spain)"), and [Aeronautical Merit](/wiki/Crosses_of_Aeronautical_Merit_(Spain) "Crosses of Aeronautical Merit (Spain)") are addressed as such. Furthermore, Knights Grand Cross of the [Order of the Holy Sepulchre of Jerusalem](/wiki/Order_of_the_Holy_Sepulchre_(Catholic) "Order of the Holy Sepulchre (Catholic)"), [Order of Saint Gregory the Great](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Gregory_the_Great "Order of Saint Gregory the Great"), and the [Order of St. Sylvester](/wiki/Order_of_St._Sylvester "Order of St. Sylvester") of the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See"), and Grand Cross of The Lion, Crested Crane, Crown, and The Drum of Rwanda, and Knights of the [Order of the Golden Fleece](/wiki/Order_of_the_Golden_Fleece "Order of the Golden Fleece"), and Knights Grand Cross of several other orders of high prestige, are often addressed as *Excellency*.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Ecclesiastical use

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Ecclesiastical use")]

By a decree of the Sacred Congregation of Ceremonial of 31 December 1930[[7]](#cite_note-7) the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See") granted bishops of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") the title of *Most Reverend Excellency* or *Excellentia Reverendissima* in [Latin](/wiki/Latin "Latin"). In the years following the [First World War](/wiki/First_World_War "First World War"), the ambassadorial title of *Excellency*, previously given to [nuncios](/wiki/Nuncio "Nuncio"), had already begun to be used by other Catholic bishops. The adjective *Most Reverend* was intended to distinguish the religious title from that of *Excellency* given to civil officials.

The instruction *Ut sive sollicite* of the Holy See's [Secretariat of State](/wiki/Secretariat_of_State_(Holy_See) "Secretariat of State (Holy See)"), dated 28 March 1969, made the addition of *Most Reverend* optional,[[8]](#cite_note-8) sanctioning what had always been the practice, except possibly for the beginnings of letters and the like.

According to the letter of the decree of 31 December 1930, titular [patriarchs](/wiki/Patriarch#Roman_Catholicism "Patriarch") too were to be addressed with the title of *(Most Reverend) Excellency*, but in practice the Holy See continued to address them with the title of *Beatitude*, which was formally sanctioned for them with the motu proprio *Cleri sanctitati* of 2 June 1957.

[Cardinals](/wiki/Cardinal_(Catholicism) "Cardinal (Catholicism)"), even those who were bishops, continued to use the title of *Eminence*.

[Eastern Orthodox](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox "Eastern Orthodox") [titular metropolitans](/wiki/Titular_metropolitan "Titular metropolitan") are addressed with the style of *Excellency*.

In some English-speaking countries, the honorific of *Excellency* does not apply to bishops other than the nuncio. In English law, [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") archbishops are granted the title of *[His/Her Grace](/wiki/Grace_(style) "Grace (style)")*, similar to a duke, and bishops are granted the title of *Lord*. The same titles are extended by courtesy to their Catholic counterparts, and continue in use in most countries that are or have been members of the Commonwealth,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] with the exception of the former British East African countries of [Kenya](/wiki/Kenya "Kenya"), [Uganda](/wiki/Uganda "Uganda") or [Tanzania](/wiki/Tanzania "Tanzania").

## By country

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: By country")]

### Afghanistan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Afghanistan")]

In Afghanistan the title *Jalalat Mahab* is used for [Sardars](/wiki/Sardar "Sardar"), or Princes of the former [Muhammadzai Dynasty](/wiki/Barakzai_dynasty "Barakzai dynasty"), who are descendants of the Afghan King [Sultan Mohammed Khan Telai](/wiki/Sultan_Mohammad_Khan "Sultan Mohammad Khan"). Although Jalalat Mahab is derived from the Arabic term *Jalalat* literally meaning *His Majesty*, it is regarded as equal to *His Excellency* or *His Royal Highness* internationally.

The descendants of [King Nadir Shah](/wiki/Mohammed_Nadir_Shah "Mohammed Nadir Shah") held the title *Alaa Hazrat* in which context *Hazrat* is Turkish and means *Majesty* or *Highness* and thus literally translated means *Higher Majesty* or *Higher Highness* and is internationally also equal to *His Royal Highness*. The King himself held the title *Alaa Hazrat Humayoon* which literally translated means *His Most Noble Majesty*, and can be equalized with *His Majesty* internationally. [President Daoud Khan](/wiki/Mohammed_Daoud_Khan "Mohammed Daoud Khan"), the Cousin of the last Afghan King [Zahir Shah](/wiki/Mohammed_Zahir_Shah "Mohammed Zahir Shah"), who acted as prime minister under his cousin held the address *Jalalat Mahab Aali Qadr Sardari Alaa* during his term as [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_minister "Prime minister").

### Albania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Albania")]

The [president](/wiki/President_of_Albania "President of Albania"), the [chairman of the Parliament](/wiki/Chairman_of_the_Parliament_of_Albania "Chairman of the Parliament of Albania") and the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Albania "Prime Minister of Albania") are referred to as *His/Her Excellency*.

### Armenia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Armenia")]

The [president](/wiki/President_of_Armenia "President of Armenia"), the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_minister_of_Armenia "Prime minister of Armenia"), the [chairman of the Parliament](/wiki/Chairman_of_the_Parliament_of_Armenia "Chairman of the Parliament of Armenia"), as well as ministers and ambassadors of [Armenia](/wiki/Armenia "Armenia") are addressed as *Excellency*, which in [Armenian](/wiki/Armenian_language "Armenian language") is *Ձերդ գերազանցութիւն*, Romanized *Dzerd gerazancutiwn*. The members of the traditional [Armenian nobility](/wiki/Armenian_nobility "Armenian nobility") are also addressed as *Excellency*, which in [Armenian](/wiki/Armenian_language "Armenian language") it is *Ձերդ պայծառութիւն* Romanized as *Dzerd paytzarrowtiwn*, literally *Your Brightness*.

### Bangladesh

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Bangladesh")]

In [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh") the [president](/wiki/President_of_Bangladesh "President of Bangladesh"), [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_minister_of_Bangladesh "Prime minister of Bangladesh"), [chief adviser](/wiki/Chief_Adviser_(Bangladesh) "Chief Adviser (Bangladesh)"),[[9]](#cite_note-9) the [speaker of the Parliament](/wiki/Speaker_of_the_Parliament_of_Bangladesh "Speaker of the Parliament of Bangladesh") and international diplomats are styled with *His/Her Excellency*.

### Barbados

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Barbados")]

The [president](/wiki/President_of_Barbados "President of Barbados"), is styled with *His/Her Excellency* formally while their informal title is "Mr./
Madam President".

### Belgium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Belgium")]

Members of the [Council of Ministers of Belgium](/wiki/Council_of_Ministers_of_Belgium "Council of Ministers of Belgium"), consisting of the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Belgium "Prime Minister of Belgium"), [deputy prime minister(s)](/wiki/Deputy_Prime_Minister "Deputy Prime Minister"), other [ministers](/wiki/Federal_Government_of_Belgium "Federal Government of Belgium"), and [Secretaries of state](/wiki/Secretary_of_state#Belgium "Secretary of state"), and also honorary [ministers of state](/wiki/Minister_of_State_(Belgium) "Minister of State (Belgium)"), are entitled to the style of *His/Her Excellency*.

The style is also used for the [governors of Belgium](/wiki/Governor#Benelux_monarchies "Governor"), which are governors of the ten [provinces of Belgium](/wiki/Provinces_of_Belgium "Provinces of Belgium"), [governor of Brussels](/wiki/Governor_of_Brussels "Governor of Brussels"), governor of the [national bank of Belgium](/wiki/National_bank_of_Belgium "National bank of Belgium") and both foreign ambassadors in Belgium and Belgian ambassadors to other countries.

Moreover, within the [Catholic Church in Belgium](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Belgium "Catholic Church in Belgium"), the ([arch-](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop"))[bishops](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") and [(pro-)nuntia](/wiki/Nuncio "Nuncio") are referred to as *His Excellency Monsignor* or *Zijne Excellentie Monseigneur* in Dutch, *Son Excellence Monseigneur* in French and *Seine Exzellenz Monsignore* in German .

### Brazil

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Brazil")]

In 1991, the [Brazilian](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") [Presidential Office](/wiki/President_of_Brazil "President of Brazil") issued a composition manual to establish the appropriate usage of the [Portuguese language](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language") for all government agencies. The manual states that the title of *Excelência* (Excellency) is the proper form used to address the president and [vice president](/wiki/Vice-President_of_Brazil "Vice-President of Brazil"), all [members of parliament](/wiki/Congresso_Nacional "Congresso Nacional") and [judges](/wiki/Judge "Judge"), among other officials.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Cambodia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Cambodia")]

In the [Kingdom of Cambodia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Cambodia "Kingdom of Cambodia"), deputy prime ministers, senior ministers, members of the [Council of Ministers](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Cambodia "Cabinet of Cambodia"), secretary of state, and members of [Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_Cambodia "Parliament of Cambodia") are referred to as "His/Her Excellency" ([Khmer](/wiki/Khmer_language "Khmer language"): ឯកឧត្ដម, *Êk Ŏtdâm*/លោកជំទាវ, *Loŭk Chumtéav*).

### Commonwealth of Nations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Commonwealth of Nations")]

Within the Commonwealth of Nations, the following officials usually use the style *His or Her Excellency*:

* The [Commonwealth secretary-general](/wiki/Commonwealth_Secretary-General "Commonwealth Secretary-General");
* Presidents of Commonwealth republics;
* [Governors](/wiki/Governor "Governor") and [governors-general](/wiki/Governor-general "Governor-general"), and the spouses of governors-general;[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* Commonwealth [high commissioners](/wiki/High_commissioner "High commissioner") (but *not* their spouses);
* Foreign ambassadors (but *not* their spouses);
* Foreign dignitaries who are entitled to the style in their own countries.

While reference may be made to the *King's Most Excellent Majesty*, the style *Excellency* is not used with reference to the [King](/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_United_Kingdom "Monarchy of the United Kingdom").

### Chile

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Chile")]

The [president of Chile](/wiki/President_of_Chile "President of Chile") and the [president of the Chamber of Deputies of Chile](/wiki/President_of_the_Chamber_of_Deputies_of_Chile "President of the Chamber of Deputies of Chile") are referred to by the style "His/Her Excellency".

### Finland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Finland")]

The [president of Finland](/wiki/President_of_Finland "President of Finland") and [prime minister of Finland](/wiki/Prime_minister_of_Finland "Prime minister of Finland") are both referred to by the style "His/Her Excellency" in international diplomacy.

### Germany

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Germany")]

The [president of Germany](/wiki/President_of_Germany "President of Germany") and [chancellor of Germany](/wiki/Chancellor_of_Germany "Chancellor of Germany") are both referred to by the style "Excellency" in international diplomacy, albeit not domestically.

### India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: India")]

The [president of India](/wiki/President_of_India "President of India") and [governors](/wiki/Governor "Governor") of Indian states are addressed as Rashtrapati Mahoday/Rashtrapati Mahodaya (if lady) (राष्ट्रपति महोदय / राष्ट्रपति महोदया (if lady), Honourable President) and Rajyapal Mahoday/Rajyapal Mahodaya (if lady) (राज्यपाल महोदय /राज्यपाल महोदया (if lady), Honourable Governor) respectively.

*His/Her Excellency*, a custom dating from the ancient times wherein the Samrāṭa and Sāmrājñī (सम्राट, साम्राज्ञी / Emperor, Empress), Generals, Kings, Ambassadors were addressed. A classic example is addressing Devavrata ([Bhishma](/wiki/Bhishma "Bhishma")) as महामहिम भीष्म (His/Your Excellency Bhishma) in [Mahabharata](/wiki/Mahabharata "Mahabharata").

However the Constitution makers[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] approved will discontinue "ancient era" styles of Mahamahim. The same release states that in [English](/wiki/English_language "English language") (which is the other language in which subsidiary official communications are released in the Central [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India") in its capacity of Sahayak Rajbhasha: Subsidiary Officiating language) the style Honourable shall replace the erstwhile His/Her/Your Excellency. The newer style will be *Honourable*.

The corresponding changes in releases from the President's Secretariat shall be from Mahamahim to Rashtrapati[-ji](/wiki/-ji "-ji").[[12]](#cite_note-12) The release also talks about the styles of other dignitaries, like [governors](/wiki/Governors "Governors"). "Hon'ble" will be used before the titles "president" and "governor", while the traditional honorifics *[Shri](/wiki/Sri "Sri")* or *Smt.* (*Shrimati*) should precede the name.

However, "Excellency" will continue to be used, only for interaction of leaders with foreign dignitaries and foreign dignitaries with Indian leaders as is customary international practice.

### Ireland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Ireland")]

The [president of Ireland](/wiki/President_of_Ireland "President of Ireland") is addressed as *Your Excellency* or in the Irish language, *a Shoilse* (literally, "brightness ([voc](/wiki/Vocative_case "Vocative case"))".[[13]](#cite_note-13) Alternatively, one may address the president simply as *President* or in the Irish language *a Uachtaráin*.

### Italy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Italy")]

The [president of Italy](/wiki/President_of_Italy "President of Italy") and the [prime minister of Italy](/wiki/Prime_minister_of_Italy "Prime minister of Italy") (officially called President of the Council of Ministers) are referred to as "His/Her Excellency" in international diplomacy. Furthermore, the terms "president" and "premier" are used as well. This latter is an informal and common address for the prime minister of Italy. For both institutional charges, the term "president" is usually exploited in formal and informal situations.

### Jordan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Jordan")]

Like many countries that once formed part of the Ottoman Empire, *His/Her Excellency* is used as the style for those with the title of [Bey](/wiki/Bey "Bey") or [Pasha](/wiki/Pasha "Pasha"). In Arabic the latter titles are often included between the first and last names of the holder, while in English the titles are not usually included and the style of *His/Her Excellency* is used on its own. Those styled this way include government ministers, senior military officers, and the husbands and children of Princesses.

### Kenya

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Kenya")]

The [president of Kenya](/wiki/President_of_Kenya "President of Kenya") is referred to as "His/Her Excellency".

The governors of the counties and diplomats are also referred to as "His/Her Excellency".

### Malaysia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Malaysia")]

The [governors](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertua_Negeri "Yang di-Pertua Negeri") (*Yang di-Pertua Negeri*) of [Melaka](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertua_Negeri_Melaka "Yang di-Pertua Negeri Melaka"), [Penang](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertua_Negeri_of_Penang "Yang di-Pertua Negeri of Penang"), [Sabah](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertua_Negeri_of_Sabah "Yang di-Pertua Negeri of Sabah"), and [Sarawak](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertua_Negeri_of_Sarawak "Yang di-Pertua Negeri of Sarawak") are referred to as "His Excellency" (Tuan Yang Terutama). High commissioners and ambassadors are also referred to as "His/Her Excellency" (Tuan/Puan Yang Terutama).

### Monaco

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Monaco")]

The [Minister of State](/wiki/Minister_of_State "Minister of State"), who acts as the Prime Minister of the Principality, is referred to as "His Excellency".

### Myanmar

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Myanmar")]

The [president of Myanmar](/wiki/President_of_Myanmar "President of Myanmar"), [first lady](/wiki/First_Lady_of_Myanmar "First Lady of Myanmar"), [state counsellor](/wiki/State_Counsellor_of_Myanmar "State Counsellor of Myanmar"), vice-presidents of Myanmar, [speaker of the Pyidaungsu Hluttaw](/wiki/Speaker_of_the_Assembly_of_the_Union_of_Myanmar "Speaker of the Assembly of the Union of Myanmar"), [speaker of the House of Nationalities](/wiki/Speaker_of_the_House_of_Nationalities "Speaker of the House of Nationalities"), [speaker of the House of Representatives of Myanmar](/wiki/Speaker_of_the_House_of_Representatives_of_Myanmar "Speaker of the House of Representatives of Myanmar"), governor of the [Central Bank of Myanmar](/wiki/Central_Bank_of_Myanmar "Central Bank of Myanmar"), members of the [Cabinet of Myanmar](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Myanmar "Cabinet of Myanmar"), [chief ministers of states and regions of Myanmar](/wiki/Chief_Ministers_of_States_and_Regions_of_Myanmar "Chief Ministers of States and Regions of Myanmar"), mayors and [ambassadors](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_of_Myanmar "List of diplomatic missions of Myanmar") are referred to as "His/Her Excellency" while justices of the [Supreme Court of Myanmar](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_Myanmar "Supreme Court of Myanmar") are referred to as "The Honourable".

### The Netherlands

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: The Netherlands")]

Members of the [cabinet of the Netherlands](/wiki/Cabinet_of_the_Netherlands "Cabinet of the Netherlands") ([prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_the_Netherlands "Prime Minister of the Netherlands"), [deputy prime minister(s)](/wiki/Deputy_Prime_Minister_of_the_Netherlands "Deputy Prime Minister of the Netherlands"), other [ministers](/wiki/Ministries_of_the_Netherlands "Ministries of the Netherlands"), and [state secretaries](/wiki/Undersecretary#Netherlands "Undersecretary")), but also honorary [ministers of state](/wiki/Minister_of_State_(Netherlands) "Minister of State (Netherlands)"), are entitled to the style of "His/Her Excellency".[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The style is also used for the executive officers of the Dutch royal court (great officers of the Royal House, [hofmarschall](/wiki/Hofmarschall "Hofmarschall"), [equerries](/wiki/Equerries "Equerries")), governors of the [constituent countries of the Kingdom of the Netherlands](/wiki/Kingdom_of_the_Netherlands#Constituent_countries "Kingdom of the Netherlands") ([Aruba](/wiki/Governor_of_Aruba "Governor of Aruba"), [Curaçao](/wiki/Governor_of_Cura%C3%A7ao "Governor of Curaçao"), and [Sint Maarten](/wiki/Governor_of_Sint_Maarten "Governor of Sint Maarten")), ambassadors (both foreign ambassadors in the Netherlands and Dutch ambassadors to other countries), judges of the [International Court of Justice](/wiki/International_Court_of_Justice "International Court of Justice"), and [flag-](/wiki/Flag-officer "Flag-officer") and [general officers](/wiki/General_officer "General officer") of three-star rank and above ([generals](/wiki/General "General"), [lieutenant-generals](/wiki/Lieutenant-general "Lieutenant-general"), [lieutenant-admirals](/wiki/Lieutenant-admiral "Lieutenant-admiral"), and [vice-admirals](/wiki/Vice-admiral "Vice-admiral")).

Moreover, within the [Catholic Church in the Netherlands](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_the_Netherlands "Catholic Church in the Netherlands"), the ([arch-](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop"))[bishops](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") and [(pro-)nuntia](/wiki/Nuncio "Nuncio") are referred to as "His Most Reverend Excellency" (i.e. in Dutch as *Zijne Hoogwaardige Excellentie*).

### Nigeria

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Nigeria")]

The [president of Nigeria](/wiki/President_of_Nigeria "President of Nigeria") and [vice-president of Nigeria](/wiki/Vice-president_of_Nigeria "Vice-president of Nigeria") share the style "His/Her Excellency" with the various governors and their deputies of the country's regional states as well as their wives.

### Peru

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Peru")]

The [president of Peru](/wiki/President_of_Peru "President of Peru") is referred to as "His/Her Excellency" (In [Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"): *Su Excelencia*) if in diplomatic context.

### Philippines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Philippines")]

The [president](/wiki/President_of_the_Philippines "President of the Philippines") ([Filipino](/wiki/Filipino_language "Filipino language"): *pangulo*; [Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language_in_the_Philippines "Spanish language in the Philippines") and colloquially: *presidente*) is addressed in [English](/wiki/English_language_in_the_Philippines "English language in the Philippines") as "Your Excellency" and "Sir" or "Ma'am" thereafter, and is referred to "His/Her Excellency". The president can also less formally be addressed as "Mister/Madam President". In Filipino, the president may be referred to with the more formal title of "*Ang Mahál na Pangulo*", with "*mahál*" connoting greatness and high social importance.[[nb 1]](#cite_note-15)

[Populist](/wiki/Populist "Populist") president [Rodrigo Duterte](/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte "Rodrigo Duterte") has expressed dislike for the traditional title. After assuming office in June 2016, he ordered that the title, along with all honorifics, be dropped from official communications, events, and materials but instead, he be addressed only as "Mayor" since people are already used to calling him as such due to Duterte being the longest-serving [mayor of Davao City](/wiki/Mayor_of_Davao_City "Mayor of Davao City") and that his cabinet officials only be addressed as "Secretary". Other government officials followed suit by abandoning use of "The Honorable".[[15]](#cite_note-16) However, despite the prior unofficial abandonment, the president continues to be addressed as "Excellency" in formal correspondences and petitions, either verbally or written.

All other local and national government officials are styled "[The Honorable](/wiki/The_Honorable#Philippines "The Honorable")"; both titles, however, may be glossed in Filipino as *Ang Kagalang-galang*.

### Portugal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Portugal")]

In [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), the proper style of the [president](/wiki/President_of_Portugal "President of Portugal") is "His/Her Excellency" ([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): Sua Excelência).

### Somalia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Somalia")]

The [president of Somalia](/wiki/President_of_Somalia "President of Somalia") is referred to as "His/Her Excellency" or "Jaale". Jaale was also a title used by armed forces staff officers of all branches, especially in the Aden Adde-Shermarke Era, and the Barre Era but has now is rare and has become a title for civil servants and senior government secretaries.

### South Africa

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: South Africa")]

The [president of South Africa](/wiki/President_of_South_Africa "President of South Africa") (and historically the [state president of the South African Republic](/wiki/State_President_of_the_South_African_Republic "State President of the South African Republic")), is (and was) referred to as "His/Her Excellency" if in a formal context.

### South Korea

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: South Korea")]

The [president of South Korea](/wiki/President_of_South_Korea "President of South Korea") is referred to as "His/Her Excellency" ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 각하, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Korean "Romanization of Korean"): *gagha*) if in a formal context both inside and outside of [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea").

### Spain

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Spain")]

Spain uses the title "[The Most Excellent](/wiki/The_Most_Excellent "The Most Excellent")" extensively as a formal address to high officers of the state. The following officials receive the treatment:

* The [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Spain "Prime Minister of Spain")[[16]](#cite_note-17) and former prime ministers,[[17]](#cite_note-18) current and former deputy prime ministers of the central government, current and former government ministers,[[18]](#cite_note-19) current junior ministers (*secretaries of state*) and the undersecretary of the Foreign Ministry.
* [Government delegates](/wiki/Government_Delegation_(Spain) "Government Delegation (Spain)").[[19]](#cite_note-20)
* Ambassadors and ministers plenipotentiaries of first and second rank.[[20]](#cite_note-21)
* Captain generals, generals of the army, admiral generals, generals of the Air, lieutenant generals, admirals, [divisional generals](/wiki/Divisional_general "Divisional general"), vice-admirals, brigadier generals, and [counter admirals](/wiki/Counter_admiral "Counter admiral").[[21]](#cite_note-RROO-22)
* The president, councillors, and secretary general of the [Council of State](/wiki/Spanish_Council_of_State "Spanish Council of State"), as well as the chief counsels of the Council of State.[[22]](#cite_note-23)
* The president and former presidents of each of the [autonomous communities](/wiki/Autonomous_communities "Autonomous communities"), as well as the sitting councillors (regional ministers).
* The president of the [Congress of Deputies](/wiki/Congress_of_Deputies "Congress of Deputies"), the president of the [Senate](/wiki/Senate "Senate"), all members of the Cortes Generales (although the later are usually referred to as "His/Her Lordship" (sp. *Su señoría*).
* The members of the [General Council of the Judiciary](/wiki/General_Council_of_the_Judiciary "General Council of the Judiciary"), the president and the judges of the [Spanish Supreme Court](/wiki/Spanish_Supreme_Court "Spanish Supreme Court"), the [Spanish Attorney General](/wiki/Spanish_Attorney_General "Spanish Attorney General"), and the members of the [Spanish Constitutional Court](/wiki/Spanish_Constitutional_Court "Spanish Constitutional Court").
* The presidents and numeraries of the eight Royal Academies.
* The governor of the [Bank of Spain](/wiki/Bank_of_Spain "Bank of Spain").
* The presidents of the three Foral Deputation (provincial governments) of the [Basque Country](/wiki/Basque_Country_(autonomous_community) "Basque Country (autonomous community)"), and the president of the Deputation of Barcelona.
* The [rectors](/wiki/Rector_(academia) "Rector (academia)") of the Universities (usually referred to as His/Her Excellency and Magnificence).
* [Grandees of Spain](/wiki/Grandees_of_Spain "Grandees of Spain") and their consort, as well the Heirs of Grandees and their consort
* Knights/Dames of the Collar and Knights/Dames Grand Cross of the Spanish [military and civilian orders](/wiki/Orders,_decorations,_and_medals_of_Spain "Orders, decorations, and medals of Spain")

The style "His Excellency", which has a higher connotation than "The Most Excellent", is instead reserved for the children of an Infante or Infanta, who have the rank (but not the title) of Grandees.[[23]](#cite_note-24)

### Sri Lanka

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Sri Lanka")]

The president of [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka") was referred to as *His/Her Excellency*. This was until 2022, when President Ranil Wickremesinghe banned the use. However, it is still used diplomatically.

### Sweden

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Sweden")]

The [Swedish language](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language") titles and forms of address are *Hans/Hennes Excellens* (His/Her Excellency) and *Ers Excellens* (Your Excellency).

During most of the 20th century in [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), only three officials (other than foreign ambassadors [accredited](/wiki/Letter_of_credence "Letter of credence") in Sweden and Swedish ambassadors at their posts) were granted to the style of *Excellency*: the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Sweden "Prime Minister of Sweden"), the [minister for foreign affairs](/wiki/Minister_for_Foreign_Affairs_(Sweden) "Minister for Foreign Affairs (Sweden)") and the [marshal of the realm](/wiki/Marshal_of_the_Realm_(Sweden) "Marshal of the Realm (Sweden)") (the highest ranking [courtier](/wiki/Courtier "Courtier")). They were collectively referred to as "the three excellencies" ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *de tre excellenserna*).[[24]](#cite_note-25)[[25]](#cite_note-26) In the 1970s it fell out of custom in Sweden to address the prime minister and the minister of foreign affairs as such, although they continue to be addressed as such in [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") protocol and in other diplomatic writing.[[26]](#cite_note-27) Since then only the Marshal of the Realm uses the style regularly.

Prior to the 19th century, a [Lord of the Realm](/wiki/Lord_of_the_Realm "Lord of the Realm") ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *En af Rikets Herrar*) and a member of the [Council of the Realm](/wiki/Privy_Council_of_Sweden "Privy Council of Sweden") were also entitled to the style of Excellency.

### Thailand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Thailand")]

The [prime Minister of Thailand](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Thailand "Prime Minister of Thailand"), deputy prime ministers, other cabinet members, governors and ambassadors are referred to as "His/Her Excellency".[[27]](#cite_note-28)

### Turkey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Turkey")]

In the English language, the [president](/wiki/President_of_Turkey "President of Turkey") and the [vice-president](/wiki/Vice_President_of_Turkey "Vice President of Turkey"), [cabinet ministers](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Turkey "Cabinet of Turkey"), [ambassadors](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_of_Turkey "List of diplomatic missions of Turkey"), and some other high-ranking bureaucrats are addressed as *Excellency*.

In the Turkish language, honorific titles are no longer used since the 1923 modernisation of the language; under [Atatürk's Reforms](/wiki/Atat%C3%BCrk%27s_reforms "Atatürk's reforms"). Instead of *Excellency*, dignitaries are addressed using the Turkish word for *Honorable* (Turkish: *Sayın*) followed by their office.[[28]](#cite_note-29) For example, an ambassador of Turkey would be addressed simply as *Honorable Ambassador* (Turkish: *Sayın Büyükelçi*). It is important to emphasise that this distinction only applies when speaking in the Turkish language, not in English. Additionally, in very rare [ceremonial](/wiki/Ceremony "Ceremony") circumstances, the word *Excellency* is used in Turkish. An example would be the formal [accreditation](/wiki/Accreditation "Accreditation") of a Turkish ambassador, wherein the letter of confidence authored by the [Turkish president](/wiki/President_of_Turkey "President of Turkey") would address the ambassador as *Excellency* (Turkish: *Ekselansları*).

### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: United States")]

In the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), the form *Excellency* was commonly used for [George Washington](/wiki/George_Washington "George Washington") during his service as commander-in-chief of the [Continental Army](/wiki/Continental_Army "Continental Army") and later when [president of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States"), but it began to fall out of use with his successor [John Adams](/wiki/John_Adams "John Adams"), and today is sometimes replaced in direct address with the simple *[Mr. President](/wiki/Mr._President_(title) "Mr. President (title)")* or *the [Honorable](/wiki/The_Honourable#United_States "The Honourable")*.

Nevertheless, in the protocol of many foreign countries and [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations"), the president and the [secretary of state](/wiki/United_States_Secretary_of_State "United States Secretary of State") are usually referred to as *Excellency*. Diplomatic correspondence to President [Abraham Lincoln](/wiki/Abraham_Lincoln "Abraham Lincoln") during the [American Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War"), as during the [*Trent* Affair](/wiki/Trent_Affair "Trent Affair"), for instance, frequently addressed him as *Your Excellency*.

The form *Excellency* was used for the [governors](/wiki/Governor_(United_States) "Governor (United States)") of most of the original [Thirteen Colonies](/wiki/Thirteen_Colonies "Thirteen Colonies"), and the practice formally continued after [independence](/wiki/American_Revolution "American Revolution"). For example, the term was formerly used in [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(U.S._state) "Georgia (U.S. state)") on the [state governor](/wiki/Governor_of_Georgia "Governor of Georgia")'s letterhead, the text of [executive orders](/wiki/Executive_order "Executive order"), any document that required the governor's signature, and informal settings. However, in most states the practice fell out of use (or was never introduced) and the title *[Honorable](/wiki/The_Honourable#United_States_of_America "The Honourable")* is now used instead.[[29]](#cite_note-30)

Though the U.S. president and U.S. ambassadors are traditionally accorded the style elsewhere, the U.S. government does not usually use *Excellency* for its own chiefs of missions, preferring *Honorable* instead.

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Excellency&action=edit&section=46 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-15)** The [Tagalog](/wiki/Tagalog_language "Tagalog language") word "mahál" is often translated as "love" and "expensive", but its original sense has a range of meanings from "treasured" to "the most valuable". It is often applied to royalty, roughly equivalent to the Western "[Majesty](/wiki/Majesty "Majesty")" (e.g. *Mahál na Harì*, "His Majesty, the King"; *Kamahalan*, "Your Majesty"), and at times used for lower-ranking nobles in the manner of "[Highness](/wiki/Highness "Highness")", which has the more exact translation of *Kataás-taasan*. It is also found in religious contexts, such as referring to [Catholic](/wiki/Catholicism_in_the_Philippines "Catholicism in the Philippines") [patron saints](/wiki/Patron_saints "Patron saints"), the [Blessed Virgin Mary](/wiki/Blessed_Virgin_Mary "Blessed Virgin Mary") (e.g. *Ang Mahál na Ina/Birhen*), or [Christ](/wiki/Christ "Christ") (e.g., *Ang Mahál na Poóng [Nazareno](/wiki/Black_Nazarene "Black Nazarene")*).
